word: G5198 - ὑγιαίνω - hygiaínō - hoog-ee-ah'-ee-no - Be in health

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὑγιαίνω

- Transliteration: Hugiaino

- Phonetic: hoog-ee-ah'-ee-no

- Definition:

1. to be sound, to be well, to be in good health

2. metaph.

a. of Christians whose opinions are free from any mixture of error

b. of one who keeps the graces and is strong

- Origin: from G5199

- TDNT entry: 13:08,1

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G5199; to have sound health that is be well (in body); figuratively to be uncorrupt (true in doctrine): - be in health (be safe and) sound (be) whole (-some).
